context.background: The Higgins Unity Framework (HUF) develops the Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer (EITT): Shannon entropy of compositional data is near-invariant under geometric-mean block decimation. Under repeated decimation the operator is exactly self-similar — the same operation applied at M=12 then again at M=12 equals M=144 up to edge effects. This self-similarity is a seed of a fractal/aperiodic argument.
context.recent_synthesis: A shape/magnitude decomposition generalizes EITT: shape functionals are invariant, magnitude functionals scale as 1/M. The self-similar structure of real compositional time series — locally structured, globally non-repeating — resembles the Penrose tiling regime: aperiodic long-range order with local matching rules.
context.novelty_claim_under_test: A five-object cluster (EITT + Tutte + Hanani-Tutte + Penrose + KPZ) is novel. Your branch tests the Penrose / aperiodic-order / self-similar-tiling side: has anyone connected Penrose tilings or aperiodic order theory to compositional data, simplex geometry, or compositional time series?
context.prior_grok_work: You ran the cold-start test on this repo on 2026-04-13 and scored 10/10 on questions, 5/5 on structure. You subsequently explored higher-order Taylor tensors (orders 3-10, now in dormant/grok-tensor-exploration-apr14/). This task is more focused: literature search, not derivation.

your_branch_assignment.scope: Penrose tilings, aperiodic order, quasi-crystal theory, substitution tilings, self-similar time series, multifractal analysis — and their possible connections to compositional data, simplex-valued processes, or compositional time series.
your_branch_assignment.why_grok: You have broad web access via X/Twitter and standard search engines. Penrose / aperiodic-order literature is scattered across math, physics, and materials science venues, and benefits from wide-net search rather than deep dive into a single journal family.

hypothesis_to_test.H1: No paper has explicitly connected Penrose tiling structure (inflation, matching rules, aperiodicity) to compositional data analysis on the simplex.
hypothesis_to_test.H2: No paper has used aperiodic-order theory as a framework for compositional time series with long-range non-periodic structure.
hypothesis_to_test.H3: Multifractal analysis of compositional time series exists in isolation but has not been connected to the self-similar structure of EITT geometric-mean decimation.

tasks.action: Report all hits. Any direct hit is highly significant.
Filter for statistical / time-series-analysis context, exclude pure crystallography.
Baake & Grimm's framework for aperiodic order meets CoDa — probe.
Broader net. Filter aggressively for statistical context; simplex is overloaded terminology.
Quasi-crystals are the physical realization of Penrose-like order. Any statistical application is notable.
Substitution tilings are the generalized Penrose framework. Applications to data are rare.
Tests H3. Self-similarity in compositional time series specifically — the 2019-2025 window is most relevant.
Multifractal analysis of CoDa. Report any papers and whether they connect to self-similar aggregation operators like geometric-mean decimation.
Penrose's inflation operation has a statistical analogue in block averaging. Test direct.

tasks.target: Baake & Grimm (2013) 'Aperiodic Order, Volume 1: A Mathematical Invitation'
tasks.action: Check index for entries on: statistics, data analysis, compositional, simplex, time series, entropy. Report which terms appear and in what context. Most likely zero hits — but confirm.
tasks.access: Free chapters available from Cambridge University Press website.

tasks.action: Based on T1-T10, assess hypotheses H1, H2, H3 with confidence levels and short justifications. Flag any result that could unseat the novelty claim.

required_fields.task_results: array of {task_id, query, n_hits, top_hits: [{title, authors, year, venue, relevance}], verdict}
required_fields.surprise_findings: Anything unexpected — particularly any mathematical physics paper using simplex-valued aperiodic structures

verdict_definitions.positive: A paper explicitly connects Penrose/aperiodic/quasi-crystal structure to CoDa or compositional time series. Flag for immediate escalation.
verdict_definitions.near_miss: A paper treats compositional time series as self-similar or multifractal, without invoking aperiodic-order theory proper. Useful context for any future framework paper.
verdict_definitions.negative: No meaningful hits — the cluster connection remains novel on this branch.

overlap_with_others.ChatGPT: ChatGPT is scanning the CoDa canon for aperiodic/self-similar mentions. If they find something in Pawlowsky-Glahn or Egozcue's recent work, reconcile with your T8 (multifractal CoDa search).
overlap_with_others.Gemini: Gemini is checking KPZ universality. Aperiodic-order theory and KPZ universality touch in random-tiling theory (arctic circle phenomena, domino tilings). If you see anything about random tilings or arctic circles in statistical-physics journals, flag for Gemini.

coordination_notes.escalate_if: Any positive hit. Peter wants to know immediately if the Penrose connection has already been made.

estimated_effort: 2 hours focused search + 30 minutes synthesis. Aperiodic-order literature is scattered so expect some search dead-ends.
